zotero.abstractNote: Text-to-motion generation has made significant strides, yet current methods often struggle with fine-grained semantic alignment between text and motion, leading to motion that lacks precision or fails to capture the nuances of the input description. We introduce MoLingo, a novel framework designed to enhance motion-language alignment by leveraging a motion-language model that learns a joint embedding space. By utilizing a contrastive learning objective, MoLingo aligns motion sequences with their corresponding textual descriptions, enabling more accurate and expressive motion synthesis. Our approach demonstrates superior performance on standard benchmarks, achieving state-of-the-art results in both motion quality and text-motion alignment.
zotero.conferenceName: Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
